lines: +reason = "USE must not implicitly create namespaces or databases on behalf of a session that lacks DEFINE NAMESPACE / DEFINE DATABASE authorization. The session can still set its context (so SDKs that issue USE before SIGNIN keep working), but `tx.get_or_add_ns` / `tx.ensure_ns_db` only runs when the caller has the same `Edit` authorization that `DEFINE NAMESPACE` / `DEFINE DATABASE` enforce. Without that authorization the session lands on a target the catalog never created, and the next query that needs the namespace surfaces `NsNotFound` / `DbNotFound` — the same security outcome as the older `NotAllowed`, without the side-effect of materialising resources the session was never allowed to materialise."
+# 1: A Root viewer can switch into the pre-provisioned test ns/db.
+# 2: Switching to a brand-new namespace returns the requested context
+# but does NOT create the namespace. The session retains the previously
+# selected database while only the namespace is updated.
+# 3: Switching to a brand-new database under an existing namespace
+# behaves the same way — context updated, but `new_database_auth` is
+# database; this exercises the same code path used by SDKs that send
+# `use` before authenticating, and we still must not auto-create.
+# 5: A query under the unprovisioned namespace must surface NsNotFound,
+# proving the USE in query 2 / 4 did NOT auto-create the namespace.
+# 6: Switch back into the existing test namespace with the still-unset
+# 7: A query under the unprovisioned database must surface DbNotFound,
+# proving the USE in query 3 / 6 did NOT auto-create the database.
+				// Get namespace definition. SECURITY: `USE` no longer
+				// implicitly creates namespaces for sessions without
+				// planner must not paper over a missing target by writing
+				// a fresh namespace into the catalog on the read path
+				// (which would also fail on a read-only transaction).
+				// Get namespace and database definitions. See the
+				// `Namespace` arm above for why this no longer
+				// auto-creates the target through the planner.
-				let db_def = txn.get_or_add_db_upwards(None, ns_name, db_name, true).await?;
+				// SECURITY: implicit creation of a namespace or database via
+				// `USE` requires the same authorization as the explicit
+				// namespace or database is unrestricted; downstream
+				// operations against a non-existent resource surface
+				// silently auto-created resource the session was never
+				let create_ns = if let Some(ns_name) = use_ns.as_deref() {
+				let create_db = if let Some(db_name) = use_db.as_deref() {
+							// Without this gate, a namespace-level Editor —
+							// including one on a stale token whose
+							// namespace has since been dropped — could
+							// recreate the parent namespace as a side
+							// effect of `USE NS dropped DB anything` or
+	/// SECURITY: `USE NS` implicitly creates the namespace when it does
+	/// so the same authorization must gate the materialization step in
+	/// `USE`. Returns `true` if the caller may proceed with
+	/// `get_or_add_ns` — either because the namespace already exists
+	/// (in which case the call is a no-op lookup) or because the caller
+	/// has the necessary authorization. Returns `false` when the
+	/// namespace does not exist *and* the caller lacks permission;
+	/// callers that still want to set the session context for a later
+	/// authenticated step (the typical pre-signin RPC `USE` pattern)
+	/// can do so without triggering creation. See `SECURITY_GUIDE.md`
+	/// for the database half of `USE`. Implicit creation requires the
+	/// AND the parent namespace must already exist or the caller must
+	/// also be authorized to create it — `ensure_ns_db` is
+	/// `get_or_add_ns` the parent when it is missing
+	/// namespace-level Editor on a stale token (the namespace was
+	/// dropped after the token was issued) could recreate the parent
+	/// namespace as a side effect of `USE NS dropped DB anything`.
+		// Block the upwards-create side effect: if the parent namespace
+		// is missing and the caller can't create namespaces, refuse.
+		// SECURITY: `process_use` may be called before the caller has
+		// authenticated (e.g. SDKs that call `use` before `signin`). To
+		// preserve that pattern without re-opening the bypass that this
+		// guard closes, we set the session context but only commit
+		// creation when the caller has authorization for it. Callers
+		// without permission end up with a session that targets a
+		// resource that may not exist; downstream operations surface a
+		// auto-created namespace they should not have been able to
+			// SECURITY: SDKs commonly call `use` before `signin`, so we
+			// set the session context even when the caller cannot
+			// itself is gated on that authorization; downstream
+			// operations against a non-existent namespace surface a
+			// clean `NsNotFound` rather than a silently auto-created
+			// resource that the session was never allowed to create.
"Cannot set database without first selecting a namespace".to_string(),
// Permissions clause doesn't allow to query the table
-				// Not allowed to create a table
+				// Not allowed to create a record. `USE` no longer auto-creates
+				// the target namespace/database when the session lacks the
+				// `CREATE` on a previously-unprovisioned target surfaces a
+				// `NotFound` error here instead of the older `NotAllowed`.
+				// Both satisfy the test's intent that the session cannot
+//! Regression tests for the security gate on implicit `DEFINE NAMESPACE`
+//! via USE in non-strict mode must require the same authorization as
+/// Issue `INFO FOR ROOT` as an owner and return the rendered structure.
+/// Used to assert which namespaces exist after a `USE` attempt.
+	let mut resp = ds.execute("INFO FOR ROOT", &owner, None).await?;
+/// Regression: a namespace-level Editor whose namespace has been
+/// dropped (or never existed) must NOT be able to re-materialize that
+/// namespace via the upwards path of `ensure_ns_db`. The asymmetric
+/// case — the database authorization check passes because the session
+/// level matches the (missing) parent namespace — is what makes this a
+/// real bypass distinct from the simpler "viewer can't create anything"
+/// case covered in the language-test reproduction.
+	// Issue an Editor at Level::Namespace("stale_ns") — equivalent to a
+	// token signed at a time when "stale_ns" existed. The namespace was
+	// never created in this datastore, so this represents the "namespace
+	// was dropped after token issue" state.
+	// `USE NS stale_ns DB anything` via SurrealQL. Pre-fix this would
+	// parent NS as a side effect. The fix gates database materialization
+	// on the parent NS either existing or the caller having `Edit` on
+	ds.execute("USE NS stale_ns DB anything", &sess, None).await?;
+		"USE must not silently re-create a namespace the caller cannot DEFINE; INFO FOR ROOT mentioned `stale_ns`: {info}",
+/// Same property for the `Datastore::process_use` entry point — exercised
+		"process_use must not silently re-create the parent namespace; INFO FOR ROOT mentioned `stale_ns`: {info}",
+/// And the same property for an anonymous session with auth enabled — the
+/// classic PoC case. The pre-signin RPC pattern still works (context is
+/// set), but no resources are created.
+		"anonymous USE must not auto-create namespace when auth is enabled; INFO FOR ROOT mentioned `anon_ns`: {info}",
+/// Sanity check: a Root-level Editor (a user who legitimately has
+/// `Edit` on `Namespace`@`Root`) CAN still materialize via `USE` — the
+/// fix is targeted at unauthorized creation, not "all creation".
+		"Root Editor must still be able to auto-create namespaces via USE; INFO FOR ROOT did not mention `fresh_ns`: {info}",
+		// `use_ns`/`use_db` set the session context but no longer
+		// implicitly create the namespace/database for an unauthenticated
+		// session — that auto-creation now requires the same authorization
// The client needs to sign in before it can access anything
db.signin(creds).await.expect("client should be able to sign in");
-		// After the sign in, the client has access to everything
+		// Re-issue `use_ns`/`use_db` now that the session has the
+		// authority to create the namespace/database it targets.
+		// After the sign in and re-issued `use`, the client has access to
db.query("INFO FOR ROOT").await.unwrap().check().expect("client should have access to KV");
db.query("INFO FOR NS").await.unwrap().check().expect("client should have access to NS");
db.query("INFO FOR DB").await.unwrap().check().expect("client should have access to DB");
